findings.issue: TDD Anchor set to N/A is well-justified for Task 10. Tech-spec § Testing Strategy explicitly scopes out Compose UI tests project-wide (L250-266: 'Compose UI-tests вне scope проекта в принципе'; E2E: None; integration: None). No Compose UI test infrastructure exists in the module, and adding androidx.compose.ui.test + navigation-testing for a 32-line NavHost wiring file would contradict the spike/throwaway architecture rationale in user-spec D8. The 10-step on-device user checklist (tasks/10.md L90-101) maps 1:1 onto the wiring surface introduced in SanctumApp.kt: step 1 verifies SanctumApp() mounted as setContent (start destination = model_manager), step 6 exercises composable("model_manager") { onLoad = navigate("chat/$modelName") } + string-arg encoding through navArgument(NavType.StringType), steps 7-9 verify ChatScreen receives the parsed modelName and streaming works, step 10 verifies popBackStack() returns to ModelManagerScreen with engine cleanup — which is the regression vector most likely to break from a wiring error. Coverage of the diff's behavioral surface is complete.
findings.recommendation: Keep N/A. Optional (non-blocking) improvements for Phase 2 when Compose test infra is added: (a) a single smoke test asserting NavHost startDestination == "model_manager" and that both route strings are registered; (b) a route-parsing test that navigates to chat/test-model-name and asserts backStackEntry.arguments.getString("modelName") == "test-model-name". Neither is warranted now given tech-spec constraints.

findings.issue: The null-branch `?: return@composable` on `entry.arguments?.getString("modelName")` is unreachable via the onLoad UI path (navigate("chat/$modelName") always supplies the argument, and no deep links are registered per task spec). The user checklist therefore cannot exercise this branch. This is defensive-only code, not a coverage gap in the behavioral sense — and per tech-spec there is no test layer to cover it anyway.
findings.recommendation: Leave as-is. It is the hint pattern from tasks/10.md L126 (safer than throw on theoretical race). Do NOT add a test for it — writing a test just to hit dead-defensive code would be redundant testing per test-master guide.

summary: N/A TDD anchor is consistent with tech-spec (no Compose UI test infra, per-project decision). The 10-step on-device user checklist covers every navigation wiring surface in the 32-line SanctumApp.kt diff — start destination, onLoad→navigate, navArgument parsing, popBackStack cleanup. No automated tests warranted; no blockers.
